page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, the processor features 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or hardware GPU accelerators, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth. The server achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 98074.28 and a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20886.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 6189.87 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 121.21 GB/sec. Software compilation using Clang scored in the top tier at 59675.0. However, single-core performance remains average, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1549.0, and text generation for small LLM models is poor at 30.21 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.metal offers high resource density and strong multi-core performance per unit of resource, typical of the Graviton ARM architecture. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, but the 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth mitigates potential I/O bottlenecks. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and network-intensive applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 architecture. Operating at 2.6 GHz, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server is designed for high-throughput networking, offering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark results demonstrate strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 20886.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 98074.28. It also exhibits top-tier memory performance, with cached read-only bandwidth reaching 6189.87 GB/sec. While single-core performance and LLM text generation are average to poor, its high multi-threaded capacity and network throughput make it highly efficient for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and network-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 64 Graviton physical cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for compute workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server delivering 64 dedicated cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Strong multi-core performance with top-tier software compilation benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and web serving
